summary: Fiery second set with Watchtower, The Other One; strong mid‑set jams
blurb: The show shines on a high‑energy second set featuring The Other One, All Along The Watchtower and Chimes of Freedom. Standout moments also include Looks Like Rain, Shakedown Street and a scorching Not Fade Away. Overall the performance is praised as emotionally resonant despite mixed recording quality.
review: July 26, 1987 at Anaheim Stadium captured the Dead in top form during an expansive four‑set marathon. After a lively first set that wandered a bit, the second set exploded with The Other One’s relentless drive, a searing All Along The Watchtower and the hauntingly beautiful Chimes of Freedom, all buoyed by tight, dynamic drumming from Mickey and Billy. Highlights from the earlier set—Looks Like Rain, Shakedown Street and an inventive Not Fade Away—showcase Jerry’s genius‑level guitar work, Phil’s thunderous bass “sonic bombs,” and Bob’s scorching vocal delivery. The third Dylan‑focused set added a raw, off‑key charm, while the encore closed with Touch of Grey’s uplifting optimism. Fans should gravitate toward the Miller soundboard capture for its crystal‑clear mix that lets every nuance shine.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Three recordings describe the show positively (fiery second set, emotional resonance, standout songs) while negative sentiment relates only to recording fidelity. No consistent performance criticism appears, so a strong positive adjustment is applied.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard source with clear, balanced and pristine audio that captures the full energy of the performance.

key_highlights: High‑energy second set featuring The Other One, Watchtower and Chimes of Freedom.
Standout performances of Looks Like Rain, Shakedown Street and Not Fade Away.
Emotional psychedelic vibe throughout, especially in the first two sets.

band_performance.Jerry: Inventive and ‘genius’ on Not Fade Away, delivering ace guitar work throughout the show.
band_performance.Phil: Provides powerful bass impact, described as delivering “sonic bombs” on Shakedown Street.
band_performance.Bob: Scorching performance of Looks Like Rain; noted for playing with a pink guitar and Madonna shirt.
band_performance.Mickey: Tight, dynamic drumming that drives the high‑energy sections.
band_performance.Billy: Tight, dynamic drumming that drives the high‑energy sections.
